title: Easy questions
wikitext:
[]: Easy questions.             Quote Post by Adamski » Thu Jun 17, 2010 2:57 am Easiest question first: What are the differences between the available versions of rebuild? What makes 2.22 different from 2.0? and so on. Also: I really don't feel like re watching all of the original series again so, is it just me, or did some of the angel attacks get skipped? Is my chronological memory askew or is the order just being shuffled for the rebuild? I lurked a bit for these answers, but didn't find anything. If they've been discussed before, I won't complain if the topics are linked and the thread is locked. Thanks in advance.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 17, 2010 3:00 am From what I understand, 1.01 adds a couple of scenes to the beginning of 1.0, but adds a darkness issue. 1.11, the BD version, is identical to 1.01, but is actually visible. 2.22 adds several scenes as well. Unfortunately, as this topic is not marked spoilers, I have no inclination to discuss them. And yes, Angels are attacking differently this time. This is apparent as early as the fact that Sachiel is the FOURTH Angel, and Fuyutsuki's line in 1.0 leaves room for only 13 Angels TOTAL, whereas NGE had 17. Not gonna say any more than that, because, again, spoilers.

[]:             Quote Post by Adamski » Thu Jun 17, 2010 3:02 am That's all I needed to know. Thanks. Back to lurking...

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 17, 2010 3:05 am Oh! I believe that 2.22 (and probably 1.01) also cleaned up some animation.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Thu Jun 17, 2010 3:06 am Dont forget that 2.22 leads up to ep 23 in the series; as with Fuyutsuki's "That leaves 8 more" line they did skip a lot of angels, as well as merge a few. They kept the most memorable ones (I just dont see the falling angel to be the memorable - they just did a good job at making it look cool in 2.0)

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Thu Jun 17, 2010 6:11 pm Eva 02 wrote: Dont forget that 2.22 leads up to ep 23 in the series; as with Fuyutsuki's "That leaves 8 more" line they did skip a lot of angels, as well as merge a few. They kept the most memorable ones (I just dont see the falling angel to be the memorable - they just did a good job at making it look cool in 2.0)

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jun 17, 2010 6:13 pm SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jun 17, 2010 9:09 pm Wait, but... SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Thu Jun 17, 2010 9:40 pm Even if he does refer to that,we clearly can see that 2.22 goes up to the events of episode 19 in the series.3.0 will most likely go up or past the events of episode 25-26 in the series with final being all new.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jun 17, 2010 9:57 pm Eva 04 wrote: Wrong. 2.22 leads up to the end of episode 19 in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jun 17, 2010 10:17 pm 1.01 had some tweaks but darkness issues while 1.11 adds a few new scenes with fixed darkness. 2.22 bypassed 2.02 which is why the new scenes are there. (1.01 having darkness issues was apparently a mistake.)

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Fri Jun 18, 2010 10:29 am (I just dont see the falling angel to be the memorable - they just did a good job at making it look cool in 2.0)

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Fri Jun 18, 2010 3:21 pm Ah, that's right. Mr. Toilet Paper isnt the the final episode b4 kawarou, should be tho... I still think its not entirely untrue that 2.0 ends with 23, the main reason I said that is because SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Fri Jun 18, 2010 3:33 pm We don't know anything about the next two movies, and we certainly can't say something like "it will correspond to episode 24", since everything is different. And we still don't know if there will be 4 real movies, or if the two last ones will be 45 minutes each as initially planned and combined into one movie like EoE. I think episode 21 will be incorporated in a way or another, but it will probably be very light. But it's so crucial to the understanding of the scenario that I don't see them skipping it.

[]:             Quote Post by Adamski » Sat Jun 19, 2010 1:56 am At this point, I think it's impossible to tell exactly where in the series Rebuild has left off at. So much has changed at this point that they can hardly be compared chronologically.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Sat Jun 19, 2010 2:25 am Jinroh wrote: And we still don't know if there will be 4 real movies, or if the two last ones will be 45 minutes each as initially planned and combined into one movie like EoE.
